title: Set-Theoretic Paradoxes and Their Foundational Impact
description: A flowchart illustrating the progression from naive set theory through major paradoxes to their proposed solutions, showing how these paradoxes led to the development of modern axiomatic set theory.
elements: Naive Set Theory (Free use of 'set of all sets')
Russell's Paradox (Set of all sets that don't contain themselves)
Cantor's Paradox (Set of all cardinal numbers)
Burali-Forti Paradox (Set of all ordinal numbers)
